summary: cookie-parser-legacy: typosquat of cookie-parser pulling unrelated 'moustick' dependency in place of cookie-signature
details: Package name and metadata impersonate the well-known express `cookie-parser` (author/copyright attribution copied verbatim, identical exported API). However, the dependency that backs cookie signing has been swapped: where the legitimate cookie-parser depends on `cookie-signature`, this package declares `"moustick": "^1.2.1"` in package.json and loads it at index.js:16 via `var signature = require('moustick');`, then uses it as `signature.unsign(...)`. `moustick` is an unrelated third-party package and will be installed and executed in the consumer's process whenever `cookie-parser-legacy` is required. index.js additionally imports `child_process.execSync` and defines a `String.fromCharCode` helper `g(n)` that are never referenced inside this file — unusual scaffolding for a 100-line cookie parser, consistent with payload assembly performed by the substituted dependency. Installing this package silently introduces `moustick` into the dependency tree under the guise of cookie-parser functionality.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
